content:
# Recommendations — next plan iter (post iter-055)

## CRITICAL — three of four sorries now converge on ONE missing construction
The S4b inner seam (`tensorObj_unit_iso_restrict_compat_inner`, NEW this iter), S3
(`dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat`), and S4a (`dual_unit_iso_restrict_compat`) are ALL blocked on
the same missing infrastructure: a registered **`Functor.Monoidal (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ)`**
instance (built from the existing tensorator δ = `pullbackTensorMap` and unit η = `pullbackUnitIso`),
or equivalently a B1-depth `sheafificationCompPullback` left-unitor / dual coherence cone.
- **Action:** before grinding any more individual squares, dispatch **mathlib-analogist
  (api-alignment)** on "register `Functor.Monoidal (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ)` from a built
  tensorator + unit comparison" — confirm the Mathlib idiom (`Functor.OplaxMonoidal` /
  `Functor.Monoidal` field shape, `Functor.Monoidal.map_leftUnitor`) and the exact obligations. The
  iter-055 prover, the lvb-checker, AND the planner's own S4b NOTE all independently name this same
  instance as the move that "collapses all four tensor/dual unitor squares" and supplies the dual-B1
  cone. This is the highest-leverage next step — one construction unblocks 3 sorries.
- This was already surfaced as the "FUTURE refactor" in iter-054/055 planning; it is no longer
  future — it is the live critical path. Promote it from "open strategic question" to an active phase.

## Closest-to-completion / blocked
- **S4b** — body CLOSED + isolated; closure is a one-`rw` away once the inner seam lands. The inner
  seam is the cheapest of the three infrastructure-gated sorries (a left-unitor coherence, no
  naturality-in-M, unit object fixed). Build the `Functor.Monoidal (pullback)` instance → inner seam
  closes → S4b fully closes.
- **S3 / S4a (dual flank)** — still genuinely blocked: no `pullbackDualMap` / internal-hom
  base-change cone. Same infrastructure family. Do NOT dispatch a prove lane until the cone /
  monoidal-pullback instance exists. Planner's open decision (build cone vs subsingleton route)
  stands; the monoidal-pullback instance subsumes it.
- **`trivialisation_restrict_compat` (L1304)** — telescope of all 5 squares; transitively gated on
  S3/S4a/S4b. Defer.

## Do NOT retry by tactic (consecutive-blocker guard)
- `tensorObj_unit_iso_restrict_compat_inner`: the pullback-world left-unitor coherence is NOT a
  tactic gap — it needs new infrastructure. Do not send a bare prove lane at it. (No API key for the
  informal agent was available this iter — `env | grep DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI`
  empty; not a route to lean on.)

## Reusable proof patterns discovered (also in PROJECT_STATUS KB)
- Substrate left-unitor naturality: state the inner presheaf seam in the syntactic monoidal carrier
  `PresheafOfModules (W.presheaf ⋙ forget₂)` with `(C := …)` on `tensorHom`; close via
  `exact MonoidalCategory.leftUnitor_naturality _` (bare `_` — a fully-applied form re-infers the
  wrong carrier and fails synth); combine sheafification legs by `erw [← Functor.map_comp, hpre,
  Functor.map_comp]`; close by counit naturality.
- Bridge `tensorObj_unit_iso = tensorObj_left_unitor 𝒪` is pure `unfold; rfl`.
- Apply an iso-naturality lemma across the `SheafOfModules ≫` seam **term-mode** (`congrArg`,
  `Eq.trans`) — `rw` of the equation misses the defeq-not-syntactic seam.

## Blueprint / hygiene (MEDIUM — for blueprint-writer + a cleanup prover lane)
- **blueprint-writer on `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` (lvb-checker major):** rewrite the S4b inner-seam
  proof paragraph — it currently claims closure via S2 + S4c, but the realized route does NOT use them;
  it reduces to the open B1-depth pullback-world coherence. Add an explicit "Open prerequisite"
  sentence mirroring S3/S4a; reconsider the proof `\uses{}` (drop S2/S4c edges that the realized route
  does not take); fix "modulo ϱ = restrictCompReindex" → realized conjugator is `unitRestrictIso`;
  optionally give the inner seam its own `\lean{...tensorObj_unit_iso_restrict_compat_inner}` block.
  (I added a `% NOTE:` flagging all of this at the inner-seam paragraph.)
- **lean-auditor stale comments (1 major, fold into the next prover lane on this file):**
  - L501 (MAJOR): `hKEY` `have` comment says "the sole content of the B1 crux still open" — `hKEY` is
    fully proved (L506–685, iter-053). Strip the "still open" label.
  - L838–841: STATUS comment calls `sheafPullbackUnit_forget_eq` "the single reduced residual" — it is
    fully proved. Stale residual language.
  - L1207: `(_hjι : j ≫ U.ι = V.ι)` is underscore-prefixed (convention = unused) but USED at L1219;
    rename to `hjι`.
  - L1444–1461: `exists_tensorObj_inverse` docstring uses old-scheme iter numbers (206/218/225/226)
    inconsistent with the Archon numbering everywhere else.

## Coverage debt (LOW — planner to blueprint)
- 117 `lean_aux` unmatched (was 114). The +3 this iter, all in `TensorObjInverse.lean`:
  - `tensorObj_unit_iso_eq_left_unitor` — uses `tensorObj_unit_iso`, `tensorObj_left_unitor` (defeq).
  - `tensorObj_left_unitor_naturality` — uses `tensorObjIsoOfIso`, `tensorObj_left_unitor`,
    `MonoidalCategory.leftUnitor_naturality`, `sheafificationAdjunction.counit` naturality.
  - `tensorObj_unit_iso_restrict_compat_inner` — uses `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, `pullbackTensorMap`,
    `pullbackUnitIso`, `unitRestrictIso`, `tensorObj_left_unitor`, `tensorObj_unit_iso_eq_left_unitor`,
    `tensorObj_restrict_iso_eq_pullbackTensorMap` (this one carries the genuine open obstruction —
    worth a `\lean{}` block when blueprinted).
